sections.lead: I crawled 9 pages on kozedesignbuild.ca. The site has a fast, clean Squarespace foundation with good basics in place, but two critical SEO elements are missing.

findings.title: Valid SSL + Fast Server
findings.detail: HTTPS active, 94ms response time. Clean, fast foundation.

findings.title: Strong Meta Title
findings.detail: Clear branded title in place. Google knows who you are.

findings.title: Open Graph Tags Present
findings.detail: Social sharing will display correctly with og:title and og:image.

findings.title: Sitemap Exists (8 URLs)
findings.detail: Google can discover your pages. Sitemap is properly configured.

findings.title: Single H1 Tag
findings.detail: Clean heading hierarchy with no duplicates.

findings.title: No Meta Description
findings.detail: The homepage has no meta description. Google is pulling random text instead of a crafted description that sells the click. This is the single easiest SEO fix available.

findings.title: No Structured Data (Schema.org)
findings.detail: Zero JSON-LD markup. Google doesn’t know Koze is a builder, where you’re based, or what services you offer. Adding LocalBusiness schema would immediately improve local search presence.

findings.title: 75% of Images Missing Alt Text
findings.detail: 6 of 8 homepage images have no alt text. Across the full site, this means your project photography is invisible to Google Image search, which is a major discovery channel for renovation and design-build firms.

insight.title: SEO Bottom Line
insight.content_html: The foundation is solid (SSL, speed, sitemap, headings). Three targeted fixes would move the needle significantly: meta description (5 minutes), schema markup (30 minutes), and image alt text across all project photos. These are one-time fixes with permanent impact.

sections.lead: When someone searches “design build Pitt Meadows” or “custom renovation Maple Ridge,” can they find Koze? Right now, the local presence has gaps.

findings.title: No Google Business Profile Detected
findings.detail: Koze does not appear to have an active, optimized Google Business Profile. This is the single most important local SEO asset. Without it, you can’t appear in Google Maps or the Local Pack where 46% of local search clicks go.

findings.title: No LocalBusiness Schema
findings.detail: No structured data connecting your website to a local business identity. Google doesn’t know your address, service area, or business hours from the site alone.

findings.title: Listed on Houzz
findings.detail: Active Houzz profile with project photos. Strong authoritative backlink for a design-build firm.

findings.title: Not Found on HomeStars
findings.detail: HomeStars ranks well for “contractor” and “renovation” searches in the Lower Mainland. A profile would add another citation and trust signal.

findings.title: Not Found on CHBA Directory
findings.detail: No CHBA Fraser Valley listing detected. This is an authoritative industry backlink and required for housing award eligibility.

insight.title: Local SEO Bottom Line
insight.content_html: Google Business Profile is the #1 priority. Free, 30 minutes to set up. Add your Pitt Meadows address, service areas (Maple Ridge, Coquitlam, Port Moody, Fraser Valley), project photos, and request 5-10 Google reviews from past clients. This single action changes local visibility within 60 days.

sections.headline_html:
How you compare.
In the Fraser Valley.
sections.lead: Here’s how Koze’s online presence stacks up against design-build firms competing for the same clients in Pitt Meadows, Maple Ridge, and the Fraser Valley.

insight.title: The Competitive Gap
insight.content_html: Copper Creek is the firm to watch. They won a 2024 CHBA Fraser Valley award, have a blog, a downloadable planning guide, and an active Instagram with professional photography. Koze’s design-build quality matches or exceeds this level, but the online presence doesn’t reflect it yet. The good news: most Fraser Valley competitors are weak on content too. First mover with video, build docs, and awards wins this market.

sections.headline_html:
What you have.
What you could do with it.
sections.lead: An audit of Koze’s current photography, content, and visual assets.

findings.title: 5 Project Pages with Photography
findings.detail: Portfolio section has real project documentation. The Pitt Meadows Residence shoot (our project) averages 22+ images per page. This is strong visual coverage.

findings.title: Good Homepage Content (398 Words)
findings.detail: Homepage communicates Koze’s value proposition clearly. Above the 300-word SEO minimum.

findings.title: Strong Testimonials
findings.detail: Three detailed client reviews featured prominently. Social proof is in place and credible.

findings.title: Only 9 Pages Total
findings.detail: Small site footprint limits search visibility. More project pages, service area pages, and a blog would expand the surface area for organic traffic.

findings.title: 2 Project Pages Need More Content
findings.detail: Some portfolio pages are image-heavy but text-light. Google needs 300+ words per page to rank effectively.

findings.title: No Blog or Content Strategy
findings.detail: Zero blog posts. No organic content driving search traffic. Articles targeting “design build Pitt Meadows” or “whole home renovation Maple Ridge” would capture high-intent local traffic.

findings.title: No Video Content
findings.detail: Zero project films, zero reels, zero renovation documentation. Before/after video of renovations is the highest-performing content format for design-build firms on social media.

findings.title: No Build Process Documentation
findings.detail: Renovation transformations are inherently compelling content. The process of taking a home from original state to finished product is exactly what prospective clients want to see.

insight.title: Content Bottom Line
insight.content_html: Koze has a clean site with good project photography from our Pitt Meadows shoot. The gaps are volume and variety: no video, no build process content, no blog, and a small page footprint. For a design-build firm, renovation transformation content (before/during/after) is the highest-value content type and none of it exists yet.

sections.lead: Three moves that would have the biggest impact on Koze’s visibility, based on what the audit found.

steps.title: Google Business Profile + Meta Description
steps.description: GBP is free and takes 30 minutes. Add a meta description to the homepage (5 minutes). These two fixes alone change how Koze appears in every local search result.

steps.title: Video + Build Documentation
steps.description: Capture renovation transformations professionally. Before/during/after content for social, proposals, and the website. This is the content type design-build clients search for and competitors don’t have.

steps.title: Expand the Portfolio
steps.description: Document more projects with professional photography. Each new project page is another search entry point. Pair with a monthly blog post targeting Fraser Valley renovation search terms.

insight.title: This Is Where a Creative Partner Comes In
insight.content_html:
Every gap in this audit is something I handle as part of the Creative Partner retainer. Professional photography, video, build documentation, social content, and the asset pipeline that feeds your site and proposals. One relationship, one monthly investment.

See the full Creative Partner proposal for pricing, options, and a pilot roadmap.
